name: Wave
framing: A salvaged pair of speakers, rebuilt into a custom desktop enclosure.

spec.Source: a broken pair of Edifier desktop speakers

blocks.body: Born from a group brief to ship ten different deliverables a week for ten weeks, the WAVE Speaker brought together our interests in electronics, woodworking, audio and product design. The deliverables compounded — each week built on the last — until the final object became the culmination of a semester's range of work.

We spent weeks in materials — testing wood samples, CNC-machining, 3D-printing internal components — and researching sound and speaker design. The build emerged from those weeks rather than being designed up front.

blocks.alt: Materials and CNC tests from the early weeks

blocks.body: We started from a broken pair of Edifier desktop speakers. The drivers were salvageable; the housings weren't. We repaired them and rehoused the desktop drivers in a custom-built case — CNC'd from solid wood, sanded and finished by hand, with the internal components 3D-printed and machined to fit.

blocks.alt: Side detail — the wood grain and the driver mounting
Wave and Mantis paired on the same desk
